result:
I’d make “basic needs” a guaranteed baseline everywhere: no one goes without safe housing, clean water, sufficient food, essential healthcare (including mental health), and basic education—regardless of where they’re born.

Everything else people argue about—innovation, culture, freedom, even environmental protection—gets easier when survival isn’t on the line. It wouldn’t erase conflict or suffering, but it would remove a huge amount of preventable harm and give billions more people real agency over their lives.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
